architecture.summary: A question generation system that retrieves commonsense context relevant to input text, then disentangles question generation into two latent factors: reasoning content and verbalized expression. The model uses a discriminator to score answering complexity and applies adversarial inference to learn the disentangled factors from data. At generation time, the expressive factor is sampled from learned distributions to produce diverse questions whose answers require reasoning over disjoint contexts and external commonsense knowledge.
trained_components: disentangled question generator (content and expression factors)

biblio.title: Generating Deep Questions with Commonsense Reasoning Ability from the Text by Disentangled Adversarial Inference

claims.statement: Existing methods may produce shallow questions that can be answered by simple word matching
The proposed model aims to yield deep-level and to-the-point questions from text by simultaneously considering asking contents, expressive ways, and answering complexity

The model retrieves text-related commonsense context and disentangles key factors that control questions in terms of reasoning content and verbalized way, with independence priors and constraints imposed to facilitate disentanglement
A discriminator is developed to promote deep results by considering answering complexity
Through adversarial inference, latent factors are learned from data and diverse questions are yielded by sampling the expressive factor from data distributions
Evaluations on two typical datasets show the effectiveness of the approach
